addedBlocks:
## project
Project management CLI — init, list, doctor, status, lint, sync, register, history, templates, resume. Use project_init to scaffold new projects with AI-powered defaults. Use project_doctor for a full project intelligence report. Use project_list to see all projects across machines. Use project_sync with all=true to register all ~/Projects dirs in TrailBase. Use project_resume to continue interrupted sessions (e.g. after timeout).

attachment.content:
- designfind-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- designhivemind-search: Search the collective hivemind for relevant knowledge, experiences, and skills. Use this when you need information that other agents might have stored, like "how to handle authentication", "best practices for X", or when the user asks "check the hivemind for..."
- designhivemind-store: Store knowledge, experiences, or skills to the collective hivemind for other agents to learn from. Use when you've solved a problem, discovered a pattern, learned something valuable, or the user asks to "save this to hivemind" or "remember this for other agents."
- designhivemind-vote: Vote on mindchunks to provide feedback on their quality and usefulness. Use this after applying knowledge from the hivemind to indicate whether it was helpful (upvote) or not helpful (downvote).
- designimpeccable
- designopen-prose: OpenProse is a programming language for AI sessions. Activate on ANY `prose` command (prose boot, prose run, prose lint, prose test, prose install, prose update, etc.), running .prose or .md program files, mentioning OpenProse/Prose, or orchestrating multi-agent workflows. The skill intelligently interprets what the user wants.
- designplaywriter
- designvet: Run vet immediately after ANY logical unit of code changes. Do not batch your changes, do not wait to be asked to run vet, make sure you are proactive.
- ax
- ax-agent
- ax-agent-optimize
- ax-ai
- ax-flow
- ax-gen
- ax-gepa
- ax-learn
- ax-signature
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

aiTitle: Fix QuestionRenderer responsive sizing on mobile

content.text:
All edits applied. Here's what I tightened for `sm`/mobile (desktop sizing is unchanged via `sm:` overrides):

- **Outer card**: padding `p-2.5`→`p-2`, the left avatar shrunk `h-8 w-8`→`h-6 w-6` with a smaller icon, and the icon/content gap reduced — reclaiming horizontal width.
- **Vertical rhythm**: content `space-y-3`→`space-y-2`, choices `space-y-2`→`space-y-1.5`.
- **Choice buttons** (the main height hog): padding `px-2.5 py-2`→`px-2 py-1.5`, text `text-sm`→`text-xs` with `leading-snug` so multi-line choices stack tightly.
- **Question text**: `text-sm leading-6`→`text-xs leading-snug`.
- **Nav buttons** (Back / Next / Answer): `py-2 text-sm`→`py-1.5 text-xs`.

Each choice row is now noticeably shorter, so the four options plus the Back/Answer row fit far better in the mobile viewport. The container already had `overflow-x-hidden` + `max-w-full`, so nothing extends horizontally.

The only diagnostic is a pre-existing unused-import warning (`HelpCircle`), unrelated to these changes — want me to remove that while I'm here?

To see it live, run the web app (`bun dev` in `web/`) and view `/chat` at a mobile width.

My plan: flatten the middle box on mobile (drop its border/bg/padding, keep it only at `sm:`+), and tighten the intro paragraph.

content.text: The structure is sound. Let me clean up that lingering unused-import warning while I'm here:

The file /home/diogenes/Projects/keating/web/src/components/QuestionRenderer.tsx has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
Done. Summary of the de-boxing pass for mobile:

- **Flattened the middle "question card"** — on mobile it now has no border, no background, and no padding (`border-0 sm:border`, `bg-transparent sm:bg-background/40`, `p-0 sm:p-3`). The boxed look is preserved on `sm:`+.
- **Hid the decorative left avatar** on mobile (`hidden sm:flex`), so the question + choices expand to the full left edge instead of being indented by the icon column.
- **Lightened choice borders** from `border-2` to `border` on mobile (still `sm:border-2`) — the radio circle carries the affordance, so the heavy outline isn't needed.
- Kept the intro paragraph at the smaller/tighter `text-xs leading-snug`.
- Removed the unused `HelpCircle` import.

Now on mobile there's just one box (the outer Question card) wrapping flat content, with lightweight bordered choice rows — far less nesting and the content stretches to use the reclaimed width. View `/chat` at mobile width to confirm.

content.text:
Done. The header row is now tighter:

- **Hide/Show button** is icon-only on mobile (chevron with `aria-label`/`title` for accessibility), with the text label appearing only at `sm:`+. Also shrunk to `h-6` / `px-1.5` on mobile.
- **Header row** uses `items-center` (instead of `items-start`) with a smaller gap, and the "Question" label is `text-xs` on mobile — so the whole row is shorter and reclaims vertical space.
